evidence: The paper states "The RF, eNet, and pls models...were selected to calculate the mean importance of each marker...The variable importance of each model was scaled to be within [0,1]." However, the actual results section completely bypasses importance values, using instead "sparse Partial Least Squares Discriminant Analysis (sPLS-DA)" with "association matrices...kept all the protein-bacteria pairs with absolute association values exceeding 75% percentile." The reported "top associated markers" in Figure 4 are selected by sPLS-DA associations, and conclusions about biomarker signatures are based on differential abundance and sPLS-DA correlations, never on the calculated importance scores.
